title: dianthus leucophaeus
intro: Dianthus leucophaeus, also known as the white-flowered pink grass pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Dianthus leucophaeus are said to include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application.
